Paper Title/Study: Effect of vitamin A adjunct therapy for cerebral malaria in children admitted to Mulago hospital: a randomized controlled trial
Authors: Mwanga-Amunpaire J, Ndeezi G, Tumwine JK
Outcome / Finding: Resolution of convulsions and mortality rate
Contribution: Problem: To determine if Vitamin A adjunct therapy improves clinical outcomes in children with cerebral malaria.
Contribution: Result: No significant difference in primary outcomes, but a trend toward earlier resolution of convulsions and lower mortality (8.1% vs 16.2% in placebo).

Paper Title/Study: Major reduction of malaria morbidity with combined vitamin A and zinc supplementation in young children in Burkina Faso: a randomized double blind trial
Authors: Zeba AN, Sorgho H, Rouamba N, Zongo I, Rouamba J, Guiguemdé TR, Hamer HD
Diet Type: Combined Vitamin A and Zinc supplementation
Outcome / Finding: Reduction in clinical attacks of malaria

Paper Title/Study: Effect of Supplementation with Zinc and Other Micronutrients on Malaria in Tanzanian Children: A Randomized Trial
Authors: Veenemans J, Milligan P, Prentice MA, Laura RA, Schouten NI, van der Heijden AC, Linsey C
Food Component: Iron, folic acid, copper, and vitamins
Study Population: 600 children aged 6 months to 5 years with iron deficiency
